expectations.text: Response presents 2 or more distinct named strategy options before proceeding
expectations.evidence: Option A (Parallel with Extension Bridge) and Option B (Branch by Abstraction) clearly named before any Phase 2 work.

expectations.text: Each strategy option includes explicit effort and risk ratings
expectations.evidence: Option A: Effort: Medium, Risk: Low. Option B: Effort: Medium-High, Risk: Medium.

expectations.text: Branch by Abstraction is mentioned as a viable option given the stable public interface
expectations.evidence: Option B is Branch by Abstraction, presented with conditions for when to prefer it.

expectations.text: Agent stops and waits for user to choose a strategy before proceeding to Phase 2
expectations.evidence: Subtitle: 'Awaiting user strategy selection before proceeding to Phase 2.' Explicit 'Recommended Next Steps (Awaiting User Approval)' section.

expectations.text: Absence of existing unit tests is identified as a factor affecting snapshot cost
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'Snapshot Blocker (Critical Path)' section. Two options offered if tests can't be written.

expectations.text: Agent identifies RxJava-specific library integrations (e.g., room-rxjava2, adapter-rxjava2, rxandroid) as dependencies that need to be swapped out as part of the migration
expectations.evidence: Dependency table lists rxandroid, adapter-rxjava2, room-rxjava2 with explicit remove/replace actions.

expectations.text: A behavior-spec.md is produced capturing method signatures, normal behaviors, edge cases, and quirks
expectations.evidence: Comprehensive behavior-spec.md with all four sections: Public Interface table (8 methods), Normal Behaviors, Edge Cases, Quirks (6 items), Out of Scope.

expectations.text: Spec is explicitly presented to user for confirmation before Phase 3 begins
expectations.evidence: behavior-spec.md produced but no explicit 'please confirm before Phase 3' statement in output. Phase 3 was not started (correct) but confirmation step not verbalized.

expectations.text: Characterization tests are written to capture actual behavior before any migration code
expectations.evidence: DateUtilsCharacterizationTest.kt (335 lines) produced. No migration code written.

expectations.text: Timezone implicit dependency (using default timezone) is identified as a quirk that must be preserved
expectations.evidence: Quirks: 'isToday and daysBetween timezone sensitivity. Both methods implicitly use TimeZone.getDefault(). The Kotlin migration must use the same system default timezone to preserve behavior.'

expectations.text: SimpleDateFormat lenient parsing behavior is identified as a quirk to be documented
expectations.evidence: SimpleDateFormat lenient parsing (isLenient=true by default, overflows like month 13 -> Jan of next year) NOT mentioned. Spec mentions ParseException on format mismatch but omits the lenient overflow quirk.

expectations.text: Timezone implicit dependency (using default timezone) is identified as a quirk that must be preserved
expectations.evidence: Interface table documents timezone sensitivity for isToday, startOfDay. Normal behaviors reference system default timezone.

expectations.text: SimpleDateFormat lenient parsing behavior is identified as a quirk to be documented
expectations.evidence: Quirks: 'Lenient parsing by default. SimpleDateFormat is lenient: invalid dates like month 13 or day 32 silently overflow.' Edge case example: month 13 -> 2025-01-01.

expectations.text: Agent recommends extracting networking code to a dedicated Gradle module as preparation step

expectations.text: ProGuard/R8 keep rules referencing service classes are flagged as hidden consumers
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'ProGuard Audit (Required Before Any Migration)' section. 'Stale ProGuard rules left after migration cause silent bloat and can mask R8 optimization failures.'

expectations.evidence: 5-PR breakdown: PR 1 Module isolation, PR 2 Snapshot, PR 3 Ktor implementation, PR 4 Caller migration, PR 5 Bridge cleanup.

analyst_observations: REGRESSION in behavior-spec: with_skill scores 0.6 vs old_skill 0.8. Root cause: new skill omits SimpleDateFormat lenient parsing quirk documentation in Phase 2 snapshot guidance.
Both configurations fail the 'explicitly presented to user for confirmation' assertion in behavior-spec — shared gap, not introduced by iteration-3 changes.

strategy-proposal, module-isolation, big-bang-rollback all score 1.0 in both configurations — non-discriminating at this quality level. The iteration-3 Branch by Abstraction implementation additions did not improve measurably because old_skill also passed these.
Token usage: old_skill significantly more token-efficient (23938 avg vs 27216). New skill produces more comprehensive outputs but uses ~14% more tokens.
Time: comparable. old_skill module-isolation inflated to 158s due to permissions fix re-run — that's a measurement artifact.
